package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for cross_layer_import_violation_by_package_name: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Imports where package names clearly violate a layered direction such as `repository -> handler` or `model -> gin`.
type CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameModel
}

type CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameService struct {
    repo *CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageName(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseCrossLayerImportViolationByPackageNameDTO) error {
    var h gin.HandlerFunc
    h = func(inner *gin.Context) {
        inner.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"data": input})
    }
    _ = h
    return nil
}
